items.title: Sudokoo SK620V and SK700V Review: Silent, Stylish AM5 cooling, with a built-in display
items.description: We tested Sudokoo’s unique SK-series air coolers on the Ryzen 9 9950X3D. Read the full review to see how they deliver big performance without annoying noise levels.
items.content: Ever fired up a high-end build only to hear loud fan noise ruining the experience? Would you like a cooler that looks as good as it performs, complete with a built-in screen showing performance metrics at a glance?Well you’re in the right place, because we’re looking at two air coolers from manufacturer Sudokoo – a relatively new cooling brand that we first encountered at CES 2025. The SK620V and SK700V combine striking checkerboard aesthetics, rail-based fan installation, impressively low noise levels, and a unique digital display that actually shows useful information like CPU frequency, power draw, temperatures, and load.Let's take a look at the specifications and features of the cooler, then we’ll go over thermal and noise benchmarks so you can decide if the SK620V and SK700V deserve to make our list of the best CPU coolers.Sudokoo SK700V at Amazon for $76.49Cooler specificationsSwipe to scroll horizontallyCoolerSudokoo SK620V/SK700VColorsBoth coolers are available in black or white, with or without the digital displayMSRP$89.99 for SK700V$74.99 for SK700$119.99 for SK620V$99.99 for SK620LightingNoneWarranty5 yearsSocket CompatibilityAMD AM5Heatsink dimensions396 (L) x 120 (W) x 28mm (H)Maximum TDP (Our Testing)>242W with AMD’s Ryzen 9 9950X3D for SK620V>250W for SK700VSudokoo SK700V $89.99 $76.49ViewSee all pricesSudokoo SK620V $119.99 $101.99ViewSee all pricesWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricespowered by Features of Sudokoo’s SK series air coolers▶️ Supports AMD AM5 only, comes with a high-strength metal contact frame (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The SK620V and SK700V we’re looking at today oddly only support AMD AM5 systems, and incorporate something you don’t see very often on Ryzen PCs: a solid metal anti-bending frame. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)While a design like this has potential advantages, installation is more difficult (and riskier) because it requires complete removal of the default CPU retention mechanism.▶️ Checkerboard Matrix fins and heatsink designStay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The engineering team seems to have been inspired by DeepCool, having incorporated a checkerboard matrix design into the heatsink fins, which is said to have an impact on overall static pressure.Each heatsink (of course) utilizes copper heatpipes to transfer heat to the fin plates. The SK620V features six heatpipes in a traditional spacing split across two smaller towers, whereas the SK700V has seven staggered copper heatpipes feeding one wider tower. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)▶️ PackagingThe heatsink and fan are protected by molded foam, with the remaining parts in a smaller brown box. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The box these coolers come in is designed to get your attention, with a photograph of the cooler in the middle and contrasting backgrounds on each side: white on the left, orange and dark pink stripes on the right.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)▶️ Fans install via sliding railsThere’s more to a cooler than just the heatsink; the included fans have a direct impact on noise levels and cooling performance. While the fans on both the SK700V and SK620V are similar and installed via sliding rails, there are slight differences. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The SK700V includes only one fan, which runs at up to 2200 RPM while supporting a rated 66.29 CFM of airflow, and 3.74 mmAq air pressure levels.The SK620V instead incorporates two quieter-running fans, with a lower air pressure of 2.93 mmAq, and each having airflow rated at 56.46 CFM. The rear exhaust fan can be lifted up and down as necessary, so it doesn’t interfere with your motherboard’s VRM heatsinks. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)▶️ Digital display with CPU performance metricsBoth SK620V and SK700V air coolers have digital displays that show the frequency, load, power, and temperature levels of your AMD Ryzen CPU. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)▶️ AM5 InstallationThe installation process is both difficult and simple at the same time. It’s difficult because it utilizes a custom metal frame – which just like on Intel systems, requires removal of the entire default socket. To do this, I placed the motherboard’s backplate against my knee to keep it secure when removing the screws and socket and installing the frame. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)After installing the custom metal frame, apply the included thermal paste. If you’re not sure how to do that, we have a handy thermal paste application guide that covers the different methods you can use.The rest of the process is simple. Place the heatsink against the CPU block, and use the included screwdriver to secure it. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Once the heatsink is secured, all that remains is to slide the fans into place and connect the PWM and USB cables to your motherboard. Well, there is one last step: You’ll need to download and install Sudokoo’s MasterCraft software to configure the built-in display. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Real-world testing configuration – AMD AM5 platformWe’ve tested coolers with both the Ryzen 9950X3D and its non-V-Cache sibling, the 9950X. There are some differences in how the 9950X and 9950X3D CPUs are impacted by thermal events. While the heat output of the CCDs of AMD’s 9950X3D are relatively balanced, the 9950X I’m using has one CCD that runs much hotter than the other, with a difference of over 10 degrees Celsius in some scenarios, shown below. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)We’ve since returned to using a 9950X3D for cooler testing, as it has a more balanced heat profile, and is almost certainly a more widely adopted CPU.The benchmark results shared in these reviews may differ from others because I place an emphasis on having results that are comparable to real-world use.This means that I generally test CPU coolers inside of a closed desktop case, which increases cooling difficulty compared to other testing methods. Many tes coolers on an open test bench, with lowered ambient temperatures. This can make weak coolers appear stronger than they really are. Some publications have also used generic thermal plates to test cooling solutions. I reject both of these methods because they don’t accurately reflect real-world cooler conditions.Swipe to scroll horizontallyCPUAMD Ryzen 9 9950X3DGPUMSI Ventus 3X RTX 4070Ti SuperRAMTeamGroup Diamond Rose T-Force Xtreem DDR5-7200MotherboardMSI X870E Carbon WifiCaseTryx FLOVA F50Our latest testing setup uses the FLOVA F50 computer case from Tryx. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)This case features a unique “crossflow” fan that pulls air from the side, which the company claims is more effective than traditional intake fans. For air cooling tests, we’ve added a single Noctua NF-A12 G2 intake fan. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Sudokoo SK700V: Price Comparison $74.99View $74.99View $89.99 $76.49View $89.99 $80.99View $84.99ViewWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricespowered by
items.author: Albert Thomas
items.titleZh: Sudokoo SK620V 与 SK700V 评测：静音时尚的AM5散热器，搭载内置显示屏
items.descriptionZh:
在高端PC装机过程中，风扇噪音和散热器美观度常影响用户体验。Sudokoo在CES 2025亮相后，推出了两款兼具美学与性能的风冷散热器——SK620V和SK700V。它们不仅拥有独特的棋盘格外观和极低的噪音水平，还创新性地内置了可实时显示CPU频率、功耗、温度和负载等关键指标的数字显示屏。本文将详细解析这两款散热器的规格、设计特点、实测性能，并评估其是否值得列入最佳CPU散热器推荐名单。

**产品规格概览**
两款散热器均提供黑色或白色可选，且区分带数字显示屏（V型号）与不带显示屏的版本。SK700V（带屏版）MSRP为89.99美元（实测优惠价76.49美元），SK620V（带屏版）为119.99美元（实测优惠价101.99美元）。它们均提供5年质保，但目前**仅支持AMD AM5平台**。散热器尺寸为396mm（长）x 120mm（宽）x 28mm（高）。在实测中，SK620V可压制AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D的超过242W TDP，SK700V则超过250W。

**核心技术原理与设计创新**
1.  **专属AM5支持与高强度金属接触框架**：两款散热器目前仅兼容AM5平台，并采用了在AMD平台上较少见的**坚固金属防弯曲框架**。该设计能更好保护CPU并改善散热接触，但安装更为复杂且存在风险，因为它需要完全移除主板原装的CPU扣具机制。
2.  **棋盘格矩阵鳍片与散热模组设计**：散热鳍片采用了独特的棋盘格矩阵设计，灵感可能源自DeepCool，据称能对整体风压产生影响。均采用铜质热管将热量传导至鳍片：**SK620V采用双塔结构，配备6根传统间距的热管；SK700V则为更宽的单塔结构，配备了7根交错排列的铜热管**，理论上散热效率更高。
3.  **滑动轨道风扇安装系统**：散热器风扇通过滑动轨道安装，便于调整和拆卸。两者配置不同：
    *   **SK700V**：标配**单个风扇**，最高转速2200 RPM，标称风量66.29 CFM，风压3.74 mmAq。
    *   **SK620V**：标配**两个风扇**，运行更安静，风压较低（2.93 mmAq），单个风量为56.46 CFM。其**后排风扇可上下升降**，以避免与主板VRM散热片冲突，提升了兼容性。
4.  **集成数字显示屏**：两款“V”型号均配备了数字显示屏，可直接显示AMD Ryzen CPU的实时频率、负载、功耗和温度，方便用户直观监控系统状态。需通过Sudokoo的MasterCraft软件进行配置。
5.  **包装与安装过程**：包装设计醒目，散热模组和风扇由成型泡沫保护。安装过程“既困难又简单”：困难在于需拆除原装AM5扣具并安装定制金属框架（建议将主板背板抵住膝盖以稳定操作）；简单之处在于，框架安装并涂抹导热膏后，只需将散热器对准CPU并拧紧，最后滑入风扇并连接PWM与USB线缆即可。

**性能测试配置与方法论**
为确保测试结果贴近真实使用场景，评测采用了以下配置并在**封闭式机箱内**进行，这比开放式测试台要求更苛刻，能更真实反映散热器性能：
*   **CPU**: AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D（选用原因：其CCD热分布比9950X更均衡，且用户群更广）
*   **GPU**: MSI Ventus 3X RTX 4070Ti Super
*   **内存**: TeamGroup Diamond Rose T-Force Xtreem DDR5-7200
*   **主板**: MSI X870E Carbon Wifi
*   **机箱**: Tryx FLOVA F50（配备独特的“侧向交叉气流”风扇，并为风冷测试增加了一个Noctua NF-A12 G2进风风扇）

**技术影响与应用场景分析**
Sudokoo SK系列散热器通过结合**高效散热设计、低噪音运行和可视化监控功能**，瞄准了对装机美学、静音和硬件信息可视化有较高要求的PC爱好者及高端用户。其仅支持AM5的平台限制，显示了品牌针对AMD最新平台的专注策略。独特的棋盘格美学和集成显示屏，使其在众多散热器中拥有高辨识度，适合用于打造个性化、外观整洁且信息透明的性能主机。然而，复杂的安装过程（特别是金属框架的安装）对新手用户提出了挑战，且目前平台兼容性有限，是其主要的应用门槛。

**总结**
Sudokoo SK620V和SK700V风冷散热器以其标志性的棋盘格设计、创新的滑动轨道风扇、实用的集成显示屏以及出色的静音和散热性能（在封闭机箱内成功压制高端Ryzen 9处理器），在高端风冷市场提供了新的选择。它们特别适合追求静音、美观且希望实时监控硬件状态的AM5平台用户。尽管安装过程稍显复杂且平台兼容性目前有限，但其综合性能与独特功能使其成为构建高性能、个性化AMD AM5系统时值得考虑的散热解决方案。

items.title: New flat M.2 SSD adapter doesn't stick out from the motherboard, unlocking PCIe slots hidden under massive GPUs — JEYI's new 'ArcherX' AIC lays completely flush and supports PCIe 4.0 speeds
items.description: JEYI's new PCIe to M.2 adapter is flat and sits flush with the motherboard when installed in a PCIe slot. It basically takes up no perpendicular space, allowing an SSD to be installed even in tight spaces where the GPU is often blocking the area. The adapter features PCIe 4.0 speeds and is backwards compatible with PCIe 3.0 as well.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 11 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Adding more storage to your PC can prove to be a difficult task if your GPU is covering the M.2 slots. And if they're already filled, adding an expansion card to an empty PCIe slot can be a bit tricky because your GPU could block that, too. JEYI, a popular accessories manufacturer from China, has a solution to this problem: a flat PCIe-to-M.2 adapter that sits completely flush with the motherboard.Most AIC (add-in cards) take up perpendicular space inside the chassis because they stick out vertically from the motherboard. Modern graphics cards are often so large that they block even neighboring PCIe slots, making it impossible to add the adapter even if there's space otherwise. JEYI's flat adapter solves this by putting the M.2 slot parallel to the board, similar to an actual, native M.2 slot, so it doesn't protrude at all.Image 1 of 4A better, top-down look at how the adapter would appear inserted in the PCIe slot(Image credit: JEYI)The two different variants of the ArcherX adapter(Image credit: JEYI)JEYI "ArcherX" flat PCIe to M.2 adapter in all its glory(Image credit: JEYI)JEYI "ArcherX" adapter with an SSD installed(Image credit: JEYI)The adapter is called "ArcherX" and comes in two flavors: PCIe 4.0 x1 and PCIe 4.0 x4. Both are also backward compatible with PCIe 3.0, and the vendor teased a PCIe 5.0 adapter in the works as well. The card is made by soldering two PCBs together — on one side, you have the PCIe connector itself and, on the other, you have the M.2 slot. It has a long, thin bar sticking out with a screw mount at the end for full-size (2280) SSDs. Contrary to how it may look, the company says it's very rigid.Article continues below You may like This platypus PCIe adapter converts half-height GPUs into full-height while adding two M.2 slots for SSDs Xbox Series X|S storage expansion cards can be used on PC with inexpensive CFexpress adapters Chinese SSD maker YMTC lists its first commercial PCIe 5.0 SSD as worldwide shortage intensifies JEYI chose to keep the adapter as small as possible to ensure no surrounding componentry comes in the way. There's no price or availability yet, but considering PCIe to M.2 adapters are cheap, and JEYI produces budget-oriented accessories, we should see this drop around the $20-30 mark. If you want to see another useful PCIe adapter like this one, check out this platypus adapter that adds two M.2 slots. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: JEYI新款"ArcherX"扩展卡横空出世：M.2 SSD转接卡实现完全齐平安装，不再外凸挤占空间，可释放大型显卡下方隐藏的PCIe插槽，并支持PCIe 4.0高速传输。
items.descriptionZh:
知名中国配件厂商 JEYI 近日推出了一款创新性的“ArcherX”平铺式 PCIe 转 M.2 适配器，旨在解决现代 PC 组装中因大型显卡遮挡而难以扩展存储空间的普遍难题。

**背景与问题：**
在现代 PC 中，高性能显卡体积日益庞大，通常会完全覆盖主板上的一个或多个 PCIe 插槽，甚至遮挡邻近的 M.2 插槽。这使得用户即使主板上仍有物理空间，也难以安装额外的 M.2 SSD 或使用传统的垂直插卡式 PCIe 转 M.2 扩展卡。

**核心技术原理与创新点：**
JEYI 的解决方案核心在于其独特的“平铺”式设计。与传统的附加卡（AIC）垂直于主板安装、占用机箱内立体空间的方式不同，ArcherX 适配器在设计上让 M.2 插槽与主板完全平行。
1.  **结构设计**：该适配器由两块 PCB 焊接而成。一侧是标准的 PCIe 金手指接口，用于插入主板的 PCIe 插槽；另一侧则是一个水平的 M.2 插槽。其整体形态扁平，安装后与主板表面齐平，几乎不产生任何垂直方向的突出。
2.  **安装方式**：适配器带有一个细长的延伸臂，末端设有螺丝安装孔，专门用于固定标准尺寸（2280规格）的 M.2 SSD。尽管外观看起来细长，但厂商强调其结构非常坚固。
3.  **空间优化**：此设计彻底规避了大型显卡的阻挡问题。只要主板上有一个空闲的 PCIe 插槽（即使是紧挨着显卡的插槽），用户就可以将适配器插入，并将 SSD 平铺在主板上方，充分利用了被显卡“阴影”覆盖的闲置区域。

**性能参数与规格：**
-   **接口版本**：产品提供两种规格，分别是 **PCIe 4.0 x1** 和 **PCIe 4.0 x4**。这两种规格均向下兼容 PCIe 3.0 标准。
-   **未来规划**：JEYI 已透露正在开发支持 **PCIe 5.0** 标准的版本，以满足未来高速存储的需求。
-   **物理兼容**：专门为 2280 长度的 M.2 SSD 设计。

**技术影响与应用场景：**
1.  **解决装机痛点**：对于使用大型双槽或三槽显卡的用户，尤其是 ITX 或紧凑型 MATX 机箱的玩家，这款适配器提供了一个极其灵活和节省空间的存储扩展方案。它使得被显卡覆盖的 PCIe 插槽“重获新生”。
2.  **成本效益**：考虑到 JEYI 一贯主打高性价比配件，且市面上普通的 PCIe 转 M.2 适配器价格已较为低廉，预计 ArcherX 的售价将在 20 至 30 美元区间，对于需要额外 M.2 接口的用户而言是一个非常经济的选择。
3.  **简化布线**：相比使用 SATA 接口的 2.5 英寸 SSD，使用此适配器添加 NVMe SSD 无需额外的数据线和电源线，保持了机箱内部的整洁。

**补充信息：**
文章还提及了另一款有创意的类似产品作为延伸阅读：一款“鸭嘴兽”形状的 PCIe 适配器，它不仅能将半高显卡转换为全高尺寸，同时还额外提供了两个 M.2 插槽，展示了 PCIe 适配器在解决空间和扩展问题上的多样性思路。

目前，JEYI 尚未公布 ArcherX 适配器的具体上市时间和定价，但其设计巧妙性已为解决 PC 存储扩展的常见空间冲突问题提供了一个切实有效的硬件解决方案。

items.title: Xbox Series X|S storage expansion cards can be used on PC with an inexpensive CFexpress adapter — Speeds top out at 1,560 MB/s in Redditor's testing
items.description: You can use Xbox Expansion Cards on PC with a CFexpress adapter since these cards use a standard CFexpress Type-B connector. There are various options available such as PCIe to CFexpress or M.2 to CFexpress, and all of them are inexpensive. You just need to format the Expansion Card before it's useable, but don't expect blazing-fast speeds afterward.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter It turns out that you can use a PCIe to CFexpress adapter that plugs into a PCIe slot on your motherboard to use Xbox Series X|S Storage Expansion Cards as local storage in your PC.Despite largely identical specs under the hood, Microsoft and Sony took different approaches to external storage for their current-gen consoles. While the PS5 has a dedicated M.2 slot, the Xbox Series X|S is stuck with proprietary Storage Expansion Cards made by Seagate and Western Digital. Turns out, they're actually not completely inflexible after all, as u/Dramatic-Shape5574 on Reddit showed you can use cheap CFexpress adapters to make these cards work on a PC.Using Xbox Series X/S expansion cards as PC Storage from r/xboxThis is not exactly groundbreaking news; it's been known for a while that these expansion cards use the CFexpress Type-B connector. The standard itself uses the NVMe protocol, but it's limited to a PCIe 3.0 x2 connection. Microsoft's custom version of it, however, supports PCIe 4.0 x2. Previously, someone even made an M.2 to CFexpress adapter that took a rare PCIe 4.0 x2 M.2 SSD and converted it into an expansion card for Xbox.We're looking at the opposite situation here, turning an Xbox Expansion Card into regular storage for a PC. The OP used a PCIe to CFexpress adapter that plugs into a PCIe slot on your motherboard, giving you a female CFexpress slot in return. There are also M.2 to CFexpress adapters that connect to the M.2 slot instead of a PCIe slot, if that's more convenient for you. Just make sure to choose one that says "Type-B."500 GB Xbox Expansion Card for $99 (Western Digital only)1 TB Xbox Expansion Card for $189.99 (Seagate)1 TB Xbox Expansion Card for $149.99 (Western Digital)Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors2 TB Xbox Expansion Card for $275.88 (Seagate)2 TB Xbox Expansion Card for $249.99 (Western Digital)4 TB Xbox Expansion Card for $549.99 (Seagate only)There's only one caveat — you need to format the Expansion Card before it can be used inside Windows. It'll still be recognized, but the Xbox-specific file system won't work with any OS directly. After formatting, it's picked up as a normal SSD with read and write speeds of up to 1,117 MB/s and 1,570 MB/s, respectively. That's much slower than even entry-level PCIe 3.0 drives, but fast enough for general usage, nonetheless.The ongoing component crisis caused by the AI boom has skyrocketed the prices of memory and storage, so if you happen to have an Xbox Expansion Card lying around, these CFexpress adapters can be the perfect stopgap. And if you want to get your hands on a new Expansion Card, you're in luck because a lot of the models are on sale. We've listed multiple options above for each storage capacity in case any deal runs out.There's no difference between the Seagate and Western Digital models, but we noticed the WD SKUs were consistently cheaper than the Seagate ones. Both are certified by Xbox, so they have identical specs and performance. That means there shouldn't be a difference in their adapted performance on PC either. If any of the Amazon links above expire, you can check out the deals on Best Buy and Newegg. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: Xbox Series X|S存储扩展卡可通过廉价CFexpress适配器在PC使用 —— 社区测试显示读取速度最高达1,560 MB/s
items.descriptionZh:
近日，Reddit用户u/Dramatic-Shape5574的分享揭示了一个有趣的硬件兼容性方案：通过廉价的CFexpress适配器，可以将微软Xbox Series X|S游戏机专用的存储扩展卡（Storage Expansion Card）转变为普通PC可用的本地存储设备。这为手头拥有此类扩展卡或寻求高性价比存储解决方案的用户提供了一个新选择。

**背景与上下文**
在本世代游戏主机中，微软和索尼采用了不同的外部存储扩展策略。索尼PlayStation 5配备了标准的M.2插槽，允许用户自行加装符合规范的NVMe SSD。而微软则为Xbox Series X|S设计了专用的存储扩展卡，该产品由希捷（Seagate）和西部数据（Western Digital）生产，采用特制的物理接口，此前被认为是一种封闭的专有解决方案。

**核心技术原理与创新点**
这一破解方案的核心在于对Xbox扩展卡物理接口和协议的识别。实际上，这些扩展卡采用的是CFexpress Type-B接口。CFexpress标准本身基于NVMe协议，但常规的CFexpress卡通常仅支持PCIe 3.0 x2通道。微软的定制版本则升级支持了PCIe 4.0 x2通道，以提供更高的带宽。

用户只需购买一个“PCIe转CFexpress Type-B”适配卡（或M.2转CFexpress Type-B适配器），将其插入主板对应的插槽，便可为Xbox扩展卡提供一个物理连接平台。这并非全新的发现，此前已有逆向操作——将罕见的PCIe 4.0 x2 M.2 SSD通过适配器转换成Xbox可用的扩展卡。此次的实践则是反向利用，将主机专用配件“解放”到PC平台。

**性能参数与使用步骤**
将Xbox扩展卡通过适配器连接至PC后，Windows系统能够识别到该设备。但由于其初始文件系统是为Xbox优化的专用格式，无法被Windows直接读写。用户需要在磁盘管理工具中对其进行格式化，之后它便会作为一块普通的SSD显示。

格式化后的性能测试显示，其读写速度分别最高可达约1,117 MB/s和1,570 MB/s。这一速度远低于目前主流的入门级PCIe 3.0 SSD（通常顺序读取在2000 MB/s以上），更无法与PCIe 4.0 SSD相比。但对于一般的数据存储、游戏库扩展或作为次要驱动器而言，其速度仍然足够。

**技术影响与应用场景**
在当前由AI热潮引发的存储组件供应紧张和价格上涨的背景下，此方案为硬件爱好者提供了一个高性价比的备选存储方案。如果用户恰好拥有闲置的Xbox扩展卡，花费不高的适配器成本即可将其利用起来。此外，目前市场上多款Xbox扩展卡正在促销，其价格（如下文列表）对于寻求大容量存储的用户而言可能具有一定吸引力。

**产品选择与价格参考**
市场上在售的Xbox扩展卡容量和价格如下（均为美元售价）：
*   500GB：西部数据版，$99。
*   1TB：希捷版 $189.99；西部数据版 $149.99。
*   2TB：希捷版 $275.88；西部数据版 $249.99。
*   4TB：仅希捷版，$549.99。

需要指出的是，希捷和西部数据的型号均经过Xbox官方认证，其规格和性能完全一致，在PC上通过适配器使用的表现也应无差别。用户可留意到，西部数据版本的同容量产品价格通常更具优势。

**总结**
综上所述，通过简单的CFexpress Type-B适配器，用户能够突破Xbox Series X|S存储扩展卡的平台限制，将其转化为PC可用的固态存储。虽然其峰值性能无法与主流SSD抗衡，但在特定市场需求和价格因素驱动下，这不失为一种实用的硬件再利用或低成本扩容手段。用户在操作时仅需注意选择正确的Type-B适配器并在使用前进行格式化即可。

items.title: The RAM shortage could last years
items.description:
According to Nikkei Asia, even as suppliers ramp up DRAM production, manufacturers are only expected to meet 60 percent of demand by the end of 2027. SK Group chairman has even said that shortages could last until 2030.
The world's largest memory makers - Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron - are all working to add new fabrication capacity, but almost none of it will be online until at least 2027, if not 2028. SK opened a fab in Cheongju in February, but that is the only increase in production among the three for 2026.
Nikkei says that production would need to increase by 12 percent a year in 2026 and 2027 to meet demand. But according to Counte …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: According to Nikkei Asia, even as suppliers ramp up DRAM production, manufacturers are only expected to meet 60 percent of demand by the end of 2027. SK Group chairman has even said that shortages could last until 2030.The world’s largest memory makers — Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron — are all working to add new fabrication capacity, but almost none of it will be online until at least 2027, if not 2028. SK opened a fab in Cheongju in February, but that is the only increase in production among the three for 2026.Nikkei says that production would need to increase by 12 percent a year in 2026 and 2027 to meet demand. But according to Counterpoint Research, an increase of only 7.5 percent is planned.The new facilities will primarily focus on producing high-bandwidth memory (HBM), which is used in AI data centers. With the companies already prioritizing HBM over general-purpose DRAM used in computers and phones, it’s not clear how much these new fabs will help alleviate the price crunch facing consumer electronics. Everything from phones and laptops, to VR headsets and gaming handhelds have seen price increases due to the RAM shortage.
items.author: Terrence O’Brien
items.descriptionZh:
根据《日经亚洲》报道，尽管供应商正在提升DRAM产能，但预计到2027年底制造商仅能满足60%的市场需求。SK集团董事长甚至表示，短缺状况可能持续至2030年。全球三大内存制造商——三星、SK海力士和美光——均在积极扩充晶圆厂产能，但这些新增产能至少要等到2027年甚至2028年才能投产。SK海力士虽于今年2月在清州开设了新工厂，但这仅是三大厂商中唯一确认在2026年前落地的增产项目。

分析显示，若要在2027年前满足市场需求，2026-2027年间年产能需提升12%。然而根据Counterpoint Research数据，当前厂商的年度产能规划增长率仅为7.5%。值得注意的是，新建产能将主要聚焦于人工智能数据中心所需的高带宽内存（HBM）生产。由于企业已普遍将HBM产能优先于计算机和手机使用的通用DRAM，这些新工厂能在多大程度上缓解消费电子领域的内存价格压力仍存疑问。目前从手机、笔记本电脑到VR头显和游戏掌机，所有消费电子设备均已因内存短缺出现价格上涨现象。

history.title: Sudokoo SK620V and SK700V Review: Silent, Stylish AM5 cooling, with a built-in display
history.description: We tested Sudokoo’s unique SK-series air coolers on the Ryzen 9 9950X3D. Read the full review to see how they deliver big performance without annoying noise levels.
history.content: Ever fired up a high-end build only to hear loud fan noise ruining the experience? Would you like a cooler that looks as good as it performs, complete with a built-in screen showing performance metrics at a glance?Well you’re in the right place, because we’re looking at two air coolers from manufacturer Sudokoo – a relatively new cooling brand that we first encountered at CES 2025. The SK620V and SK700V combine striking checkerboard aesthetics, rail-based fan installation, impressively low noise levels, and a unique digital display that actually shows useful information like CPU frequency, power draw, temperatures, and load.Let's take a look at the specifications and features of the cooler, then we’ll go over thermal and noise benchmarks so you can decide if the SK620V and SK700V deserve to make our list of the best CPU coolers.Sudokoo SK700V at Amazon for $76.49Cooler specificationsSwipe to scroll horizontallyCoolerSudokoo SK620V/SK700VColorsBoth coolers are available in black or white, with or without the digital displayMSRP$89.99 for SK700V$74.99 for SK700$119.99 for SK620V$99.99 for SK620LightingNoneWarranty5 yearsSocket CompatibilityAMD AM5Heatsink dimensions396 (L) x 120 (W) x 28mm (H)Maximum TDP (Our Testing)>242W with AMD’s Ryzen 9 9950X3D for SK620V>250W for SK700VSudokoo SK700V $89.99 $76.49ViewSee all pricesSudokoo SK620V $119.99 $101.99ViewSee all pricesWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricespowered by Features of Sudokoo’s SK series air coolers▶️ Supports AMD AM5 only, comes with a high-strength metal contact frame (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The SK620V and SK700V we’re looking at today oddly only support AMD AM5 systems, and incorporate something you don’t see very often on Ryzen PCs: a solid metal anti-bending frame. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)While a design like this has potential advantages, installation is more difficult (and riskier) because it requires complete removal of the default CPU retention mechanism.▶️ Checkerboard Matrix fins and heatsink designStay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The engineering team seems to have been inspired by DeepCool, having incorporated a checkerboard matrix design into the heatsink fins, which is said to have an impact on overall static pressure.Each heatsink (of course) utilizes copper heatpipes to transfer heat to the fin plates. The SK620V features six heatpipes in a traditional spacing split across two smaller towers, whereas the SK700V has seven staggered copper heatpipes feeding one wider tower. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)▶️ PackagingThe heatsink and fan are protected by molded foam, with the remaining parts in a smaller brown box. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The box these coolers come in is designed to get your attention, with a photograph of the cooler in the middle and contrasting backgrounds on each side: white on the left, orange and dark pink stripes on the right.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)▶️ Fans install via sliding railsThere’s more to a cooler than just the heatsink; the included fans have a direct impact on noise levels and cooling performance. While the fans on both the SK700V and SK620V are similar and installed via sliding rails, there are slight differences. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The SK700V includes only one fan, which runs at up to 2200 RPM while supporting a rated 66.29 CFM of airflow, and 3.74 mmAq air pressure levels.The SK620V instead incorporates two quieter-running fans, with a lower air pressure of 2.93 mmAq, and each having airflow rated at 56.46 CFM. The rear exhaust fan can be lifted up and down as necessary, so it doesn’t interfere with your motherboard’s VRM heatsinks. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)▶️ Digital display with CPU performance metricsBoth SK620V and SK700V air coolers have digital displays that show the frequency, load, power, and temperature levels of your AMD Ryzen CPU. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)▶️ AM5 InstallationThe installation process is both difficult and simple at the same time. It’s difficult because it utilizes a custom metal frame – which just like on Intel systems, requires removal of the entire default socket. To do this, I placed the motherboard’s backplate against my knee to keep it secure when removing the screws and socket and installing the frame. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)After installing the custom metal frame, apply the included thermal paste. If you’re not sure how to do that, we have a handy thermal paste application guide that covers the different methods you can use.The rest of the process is simple. Place the heatsink against the CPU block, and use the included screwdriver to secure it. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Once the heatsink is secured, all that remains is to slide the fans into place and connect the PWM and USB cables to your motherboard. Well, there is one last step: You’ll need to download and install Sudokoo’s MasterCraft software to configure the built-in display. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Real-world testing configuration – AMD AM5 platformWe’ve tested coolers with both the Ryzen 9950X3D and its non-V-Cache sibling, the 9950X. There are some differences in how the 9950X and 9950X3D CPUs are impacted by thermal events. While the heat output of the CCDs of AMD’s 9950X3D are relatively balanced, the 9950X I’m using has one CCD that runs much hotter than the other, with a difference of over 10 degrees Celsius in some scenarios, shown below. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)We’ve since returned to using a 9950X3D for cooler testing, as it has a more balanced heat profile, and is almost certainly a more widely adopted CPU.The benchmark results shared in these reviews may differ from others because I place an emphasis on having results that are comparable to real-world use.This means that I generally test CPU coolers inside of a closed desktop case, which increases cooling difficulty compared to other testing methods. Many tes coolers on an open test bench, with lowered ambient temperatures. This can make weak coolers appear stronger than they really are. Some publications have also used generic thermal plates to test cooling solutions. I reject both of these methods because they don’t accurately reflect real-world cooler conditions.Swipe to scroll horizontallyCPUAMD Ryzen 9 9950X3DGPUMSI Ventus 3X RTX 4070Ti SuperRAMTeamGroup Diamond Rose T-Force Xtreem DDR5-7200MotherboardMSI X870E Carbon WifiCaseTryx FLOVA F50Our latest testing setup uses the FLOVA F50 computer case from Tryx. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)This case features a unique “crossflow” fan that pulls air from the side, which the company claims is more effective than traditional intake fans. For air cooling tests, we’ve added a single Noctua NF-A12 G2 intake fan. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Sudokoo SK700V: Price Comparison $74.99View $74.99View $89.99 $76.49View $89.99 $80.99View $84.99ViewWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricespowered by
history.author: Albert Thomas
history.titleZh: Sudokoo SK620V 与 SK700V 评测：静音时尚的AM5散热器，搭载内置显示屏
history.descriptionZh:
在高端PC装机过程中，风扇噪音和散热器美观度常影响用户体验。Sudokoo在CES 2025亮相后，推出了两款兼具美学与性能的风冷散热器——SK620V和SK700V。它们不仅拥有独特的棋盘格外观和极低的噪音水平，还创新性地内置了可实时显示CPU频率、功耗、温度和负载等关键指标的数字显示屏。本文将详细解析这两款散热器的规格、设计特点、实测性能，并评估其是否值得列入最佳CPU散热器推荐名单。

**产品规格概览**
两款散热器均提供黑色或白色可选，且区分带数字显示屏（V型号）与不带显示屏的版本。SK700V（带屏版）MSRP为89.99美元（实测优惠价76.49美元），SK620V（带屏版）为119.99美元（实测优惠价101.99美元）。它们均提供5年质保，但目前**仅支持AMD AM5平台**。散热器尺寸为396mm（长）x 120mm（宽）x 28mm（高）。在实测中，SK620V可压制AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D的超过242W TDP，SK700V则超过250W。

**核心技术原理与设计创新**
1.  **专属AM5支持与高强度金属接触框架**：两款散热器目前仅兼容AM5平台，并采用了在AMD平台上较少见的**坚固金属防弯曲框架**。该设计能更好保护CPU并改善散热接触，但安装更为复杂且存在风险，因为它需要完全移除主板原装的CPU扣具机制。
2.  **棋盘格矩阵鳍片与散热模组设计**：散热鳍片采用了独特的棋盘格矩阵设计，灵感可能源自DeepCool，据称能对整体风压产生影响。均采用铜质热管将热量传导至鳍片：**SK620V采用双塔结构，配备6根传统间距的热管；SK700V则为更宽的单塔结构，配备了7根交错排列的铜热管**，理论上散热效率更高。
3.  **滑动轨道风扇安装系统**：散热器风扇通过滑动轨道安装，便于调整和拆卸。两者配置不同：
    *   **SK700V**：标配**单个风扇**，最高转速2200 RPM，标称风量66.29 CFM，风压3.74 mmAq。
    *   **SK620V**：标配**两个风扇**，运行更安静，风压较低（2.93 mmAq），单个风量为56.46 CFM。其**后排风扇可上下升降**，以避免与主板VRM散热片冲突，提升了兼容性。
4.  **集成数字显示屏**：两款“V”型号均配备了数字显示屏，可直接显示AMD Ryzen CPU的实时频率、负载、功耗和温度，方便用户直观监控系统状态。需通过Sudokoo的MasterCraft软件进行配置。
5.  **包装与安装过程**：包装设计醒目，散热模组和风扇由成型泡沫保护。安装过程“既困难又简单”：困难在于需拆除原装AM5扣具并安装定制金属框架（建议将主板背板抵住膝盖以稳定操作）；简单之处在于，框架安装并涂抹导热膏后，只需将散热器对准CPU并拧紧，最后滑入风扇并连接PWM与USB线缆即可。

**性能测试配置与方法论**
为确保测试结果贴近真实使用场景，评测采用了以下配置并在**封闭式机箱内**进行，这比开放式测试台要求更苛刻，能更真实反映散热器性能：
*   **CPU**: AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D（选用原因：其CCD热分布比9950X更均衡，且用户群更广）
*   **GPU**: MSI Ventus 3X RTX 4070Ti Super
*   **内存**: TeamGroup Diamond Rose T-Force Xtreem DDR5-7200
*   **主板**: MSI X870E Carbon Wifi
*   **机箱**: Tryx FLOVA F50（配备独特的“侧向交叉气流”风扇，并为风冷测试增加了一个Noctua NF-A12 G2进风风扇）

**技术影响与应用场景分析**
Sudokoo SK系列散热器通过结合**高效散热设计、低噪音运行和可视化监控功能**，瞄准了对装机美学、静音和硬件信息可视化有较高要求的PC爱好者及高端用户。其仅支持AM5的平台限制，显示了品牌针对AMD最新平台的专注策略。独特的棋盘格美学和集成显示屏，使其在众多散热器中拥有高辨识度，适合用于打造个性化、外观整洁且信息透明的性能主机。然而，复杂的安装过程（特别是金属框架的安装）对新手用户提出了挑战，且目前平台兼容性有限，是其主要的应用门槛。

**总结**
Sudokoo SK620V和SK700V风冷散热器以其标志性的棋盘格设计、创新的滑动轨道风扇、实用的集成显示屏以及出色的静音和散热性能（在封闭机箱内成功压制高端Ryzen 9处理器），在高端风冷市场提供了新的选择。它们特别适合追求静音、美观且希望实时监控硬件状态的AM5平台用户。尽管安装过程稍显复杂且平台兼容性目前有限，但其综合性能与独特功能使其成为构建高性能、个性化AMD AM5系统时值得考虑的散热解决方案。

history.title: New flat M.2 SSD adapter doesn't stick out from the motherboard, unlocking PCIe slots hidden under massive GPUs — JEYI's new 'ArcherX' AIC lays completely flush and supports PCIe 4.0 speeds
history.description: JEYI's new PCIe to M.2 adapter is flat and sits flush with the motherboard when installed in a PCIe slot. It basically takes up no perpendicular space, allowing an SSD to be installed even in tight spaces where the GPU is often blocking the area. The adapter features PCIe 4.0 speeds and is backwards compatible with PCIe 3.0 as well.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 11 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Adding more storage to your PC can prove to be a difficult task if your GPU is covering the M.2 slots. And if they're already filled, adding an expansion card to an empty PCIe slot can be a bit tricky because your GPU could block that, too. JEYI, a popular accessories manufacturer from China, has a solution to this problem: a flat PCIe-to-M.2 adapter that sits completely flush with the motherboard.Most AIC (add-in cards) take up perpendicular space inside the chassis because they stick out vertically from the motherboard. Modern graphics cards are often so large that they block even neighboring PCIe slots, making it impossible to add the adapter even if there's space otherwise. JEYI's flat adapter solves this by putting the M.2 slot parallel to the board, similar to an actual, native M.2 slot, so it doesn't protrude at all.Image 1 of 4A better, top-down look at how the adapter would appear inserted in the PCIe slot(Image credit: JEYI)The two different variants of the ArcherX adapter(Image credit: JEYI)JEYI "ArcherX" flat PCIe to M.2 adapter in all its glory(Image credit: JEYI)JEYI "ArcherX" adapter with an SSD installed(Image credit: JEYI)The adapter is called "ArcherX" and comes in two flavors: PCIe 4.0 x1 and PCIe 4.0 x4. Both are also backward compatible with PCIe 3.0, and the vendor teased a PCIe 5.0 adapter in the works as well. The card is made by soldering two PCBs together — on one side, you have the PCIe connector itself and, on the other, you have the M.2 slot. It has a long, thin bar sticking out with a screw mount at the end for full-size (2280) SSDs. Contrary to how it may look, the company says it's very rigid.Article continues below You may like This platypus PCIe adapter converts half-height GPUs into full-height while adding two M.2 slots for SSDs Xbox Series X|S storage expansion cards can be used on PC with inexpensive CFexpress adapters Chinese SSD maker YMTC lists its first commercial PCIe 5.0 SSD as worldwide shortage intensifies JEYI chose to keep the adapter as small as possible to ensure no surrounding componentry comes in the way. There's no price or availability yet, but considering PCIe to M.2 adapters are cheap, and JEYI produces budget-oriented accessories, we should see this drop around the $20-30 mark. If you want to see another useful PCIe adapter like this one, check out this platypus adapter that adds two M.2 slots. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: JEYI新款"ArcherX"扩展卡横空出世：M.2 SSD转接卡实现完全齐平安装，不再外凸挤占空间，可释放大型显卡下方隐藏的PCIe插槽，并支持PCIe 4.0高速传输。
history.descriptionZh:
知名中国配件厂商 JEYI 近日推出了一款创新性的“ArcherX”平铺式 PCIe 转 M.2 适配器，旨在解决现代 PC 组装中因大型显卡遮挡而难以扩展存储空间的普遍难题。

**背景与问题：**
在现代 PC 中，高性能显卡体积日益庞大，通常会完全覆盖主板上的一个或多个 PCIe 插槽，甚至遮挡邻近的 M.2 插槽。这使得用户即使主板上仍有物理空间，也难以安装额外的 M.2 SSD 或使用传统的垂直插卡式 PCIe 转 M.2 扩展卡。

**核心技术原理与创新点：**
JEYI 的解决方案核心在于其独特的“平铺”式设计。与传统的附加卡（AIC）垂直于主板安装、占用机箱内立体空间的方式不同，ArcherX 适配器在设计上让 M.2 插槽与主板完全平行。
1.  **结构设计**：该适配器由两块 PCB 焊接而成。一侧是标准的 PCIe 金手指接口，用于插入主板的 PCIe 插槽；另一侧则是一个水平的 M.2 插槽。其整体形态扁平，安装后与主板表面齐平，几乎不产生任何垂直方向的突出。
2.  **安装方式**：适配器带有一个细长的延伸臂，末端设有螺丝安装孔，专门用于固定标准尺寸（2280规格）的 M.2 SSD。尽管外观看起来细长，但厂商强调其结构非常坚固。
3.  **空间优化**：此设计彻底规避了大型显卡的阻挡问题。只要主板上有一个空闲的 PCIe 插槽（即使是紧挨着显卡的插槽），用户就可以将适配器插入，并将 SSD 平铺在主板上方，充分利用了被显卡“阴影”覆盖的闲置区域。

**性能参数与规格：**
-   **接口版本**：产品提供两种规格，分别是 **PCIe 4.0 x1** 和 **PCIe 4.0 x4**。这两种规格均向下兼容 PCIe 3.0 标准。
-   **未来规划**：JEYI 已透露正在开发支持 **PCIe 5.0** 标准的版本，以满足未来高速存储的需求。
-   **物理兼容**：专门为 2280 长度的 M.2 SSD 设计。

**技术影响与应用场景：**
1.  **解决装机痛点**：对于使用大型双槽或三槽显卡的用户，尤其是 ITX 或紧凑型 MATX 机箱的玩家，这款适配器提供了一个极其灵活和节省空间的存储扩展方案。它使得被显卡覆盖的 PCIe 插槽“重获新生”。
2.  **成本效益**：考虑到 JEYI 一贯主打高性价比配件，且市面上普通的 PCIe 转 M.2 适配器价格已较为低廉，预计 ArcherX 的售价将在 20 至 30 美元区间，对于需要额外 M.2 接口的用户而言是一个非常经济的选择。
3.  **简化布线**：相比使用 SATA 接口的 2.5 英寸 SSD，使用此适配器添加 NVMe SSD 无需额外的数据线和电源线，保持了机箱内部的整洁。

**补充信息：**
文章还提及了另一款有创意的类似产品作为延伸阅读：一款“鸭嘴兽”形状的 PCIe 适配器，它不仅能将半高显卡转换为全高尺寸，同时还额外提供了两个 M.2 插槽，展示了 PCIe 适配器在解决空间和扩展问题上的多样性思路。

目前，JEYI 尚未公布 ArcherX 适配器的具体上市时间和定价，但其设计巧妙性已为解决 PC 存储扩展的常见空间冲突问题提供了一个切实有效的硬件解决方案。

history.title: Xbox Series X|S storage expansion cards can be used on PC with an inexpensive CFexpress adapter — Speeds top out at 1,560 MB/s in Redditor's testing
history.description: You can use Xbox Expansion Cards on PC with a CFexpress adapter since these cards use a standard CFexpress Type-B connector. There are various options available such as PCIe to CFexpress or M.2 to CFexpress, and all of them are inexpensive. You just need to format the Expansion Card before it's useable, but don't expect blazing-fast speeds afterward.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter It turns out that you can use a PCIe to CFexpress adapter that plugs into a PCIe slot on your motherboard to use Xbox Series X|S Storage Expansion Cards as local storage in your PC.Despite largely identical specs under the hood, Microsoft and Sony took different approaches to external storage for their current-gen consoles. While the PS5 has a dedicated M.2 slot, the Xbox Series X|S is stuck with proprietary Storage Expansion Cards made by Seagate and Western Digital. Turns out, they're actually not completely inflexible after all, as u/Dramatic-Shape5574 on Reddit showed you can use cheap CFexpress adapters to make these cards work on a PC.Using Xbox Series X/S expansion cards as PC Storage from r/xboxThis is not exactly groundbreaking news; it's been known for a while that these expansion cards use the CFexpress Type-B connector. The standard itself uses the NVMe protocol, but it's limited to a PCIe 3.0 x2 connection. Microsoft's custom version of it, however, supports PCIe 4.0 x2. Previously, someone even made an M.2 to CFexpress adapter that took a rare PCIe 4.0 x2 M.2 SSD and converted it into an expansion card for Xbox.We're looking at the opposite situation here, turning an Xbox Expansion Card into regular storage for a PC. The OP used a PCIe to CFexpress adapter that plugs into a PCIe slot on your motherboard, giving you a female CFexpress slot in return. There are also M.2 to CFexpress adapters that connect to the M.2 slot instead of a PCIe slot, if that's more convenient for you. Just make sure to choose one that says "Type-B."500 GB Xbox Expansion Card for $99 (Western Digital only)1 TB Xbox Expansion Card for $189.99 (Seagate)1 TB Xbox Expansion Card for $149.99 (Western Digital)Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors2 TB Xbox Expansion Card for $275.88 (Seagate)2 TB Xbox Expansion Card for $249.99 (Western Digital)4 TB Xbox Expansion Card for $549.99 (Seagate only)There's only one caveat — you need to format the Expansion Card before it can be used inside Windows. It'll still be recognized, but the Xbox-specific file system won't work with any OS directly. After formatting, it's picked up as a normal SSD with read and write speeds of up to 1,117 MB/s and 1,570 MB/s, respectively. That's much slower than even entry-level PCIe 3.0 drives, but fast enough for general usage, nonetheless.The ongoing component crisis caused by the AI boom has skyrocketed the prices of memory and storage, so if you happen to have an Xbox Expansion Card lying around, these CFexpress adapters can be the perfect stopgap. And if you want to get your hands on a new Expansion Card, you're in luck because a lot of the models are on sale. We've listed multiple options above for each storage capacity in case any deal runs out.There's no difference between the Seagate and Western Digital models, but we noticed the WD SKUs were consistently cheaper than the Seagate ones. Both are certified by Xbox, so they have identical specs and performance. That means there shouldn't be a difference in their adapted performance on PC either. If any of the Amazon links above expire, you can check out the deals on Best Buy and Newegg. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: Xbox Series X|S存储扩展卡可通过廉价CFexpress适配器在PC使用 —— 社区测试显示读取速度最高达1,560 MB/s
history.descriptionZh:
近日，Reddit用户u/Dramatic-Shape5574的分享揭示了一个有趣的硬件兼容性方案：通过廉价的CFexpress适配器，可以将微软Xbox Series X|S游戏机专用的存储扩展卡（Storage Expansion Card）转变为普通PC可用的本地存储设备。这为手头拥有此类扩展卡或寻求高性价比存储解决方案的用户提供了一个新选择。

**背景与上下文**
在本世代游戏主机中，微软和索尼采用了不同的外部存储扩展策略。索尼PlayStation 5配备了标准的M.2插槽，允许用户自行加装符合规范的NVMe SSD。而微软则为Xbox Series X|S设计了专用的存储扩展卡，该产品由希捷（Seagate）和西部数据（Western Digital）生产，采用特制的物理接口，此前被认为是一种封闭的专有解决方案。

**核心技术原理与创新点**
这一破解方案的核心在于对Xbox扩展卡物理接口和协议的识别。实际上，这些扩展卡采用的是CFexpress Type-B接口。CFexpress标准本身基于NVMe协议，但常规的CFexpress卡通常仅支持PCIe 3.0 x2通道。微软的定制版本则升级支持了PCIe 4.0 x2通道，以提供更高的带宽。

用户只需购买一个“PCIe转CFexpress Type-B”适配卡（或M.2转CFexpress Type-B适配器），将其插入主板对应的插槽，便可为Xbox扩展卡提供一个物理连接平台。这并非全新的发现，此前已有逆向操作——将罕见的PCIe 4.0 x2 M.2 SSD通过适配器转换成Xbox可用的扩展卡。此次的实践则是反向利用，将主机专用配件“解放”到PC平台。

**性能参数与使用步骤**
将Xbox扩展卡通过适配器连接至PC后，Windows系统能够识别到该设备。但由于其初始文件系统是为Xbox优化的专用格式，无法被Windows直接读写。用户需要在磁盘管理工具中对其进行格式化，之后它便会作为一块普通的SSD显示。

格式化后的性能测试显示，其读写速度分别最高可达约1,117 MB/s和1,570 MB/s。这一速度远低于目前主流的入门级PCIe 3.0 SSD（通常顺序读取在2000 MB/s以上），更无法与PCIe 4.0 SSD相比。但对于一般的数据存储、游戏库扩展或作为次要驱动器而言，其速度仍然足够。

**技术影响与应用场景**
在当前由AI热潮引发的存储组件供应紧张和价格上涨的背景下，此方案为硬件爱好者提供了一个高性价比的备选存储方案。如果用户恰好拥有闲置的Xbox扩展卡，花费不高的适配器成本即可将其利用起来。此外，目前市场上多款Xbox扩展卡正在促销，其价格（如下文列表）对于寻求大容量存储的用户而言可能具有一定吸引力。

**产品选择与价格参考**
市场上在售的Xbox扩展卡容量和价格如下（均为美元售价）：
*   500GB：西部数据版，$99。
*   1TB：希捷版 $189.99；西部数据版 $149.99。
*   2TB：希捷版 $275.88；西部数据版 $249.99。
*   4TB：仅希捷版，$549.99。

需要指出的是，希捷和西部数据的型号均经过Xbox官方认证，其规格和性能完全一致，在PC上通过适配器使用的表现也应无差别。用户可留意到，西部数据版本的同容量产品价格通常更具优势。

**总结**
综上所述，通过简单的CFexpress Type-B适配器，用户能够突破Xbox Series X|S存储扩展卡的平台限制，将其转化为PC可用的固态存储。虽然其峰值性能无法与主流SSD抗衡，但在特定市场需求和价格因素驱动下，这不失为一种实用的硬件再利用或低成本扩容手段。用户在操作时仅需注意选择正确的Type-B适配器并在使用前进行格式化即可。

history.title: The RAM shortage could last years
history.description:
According to Nikkei Asia, even as suppliers ramp up DRAM production, manufacturers are only expected to meet 60 percent of demand by the end of 2027. SK Group chairman has even said that shortages could last until 2030.
The world's largest memory makers - Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron - are all working to add new fabrication capacity, but almost none of it will be online until at least 2027, if not 2028. SK opened a fab in Cheongju in February, but that is the only increase in production among the three for 2026.
Nikkei says that production would need to increase by 12 percent a year in 2026 and 2027 to meet demand. But according to Counte …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: According to Nikkei Asia, even as suppliers ramp up DRAM production, manufacturers are only expected to meet 60 percent of demand by the end of 2027. SK Group chairman has even said that shortages could last until 2030.The world’s largest memory makers — Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron — are all working to add new fabrication capacity, but almost none of it will be online until at least 2027, if not 2028. SK opened a fab in Cheongju in February, but that is the only increase in production among the three for 2026.Nikkei says that production would need to increase by 12 percent a year in 2026 and 2027 to meet demand. But according to Counterpoint Research, an increase of only 7.5 percent is planned.The new facilities will primarily focus on producing high-bandwidth memory (HBM), which is used in AI data centers. With the companies already prioritizing HBM over general-purpose DRAM used in computers and phones, it’s not clear how much these new fabs will help alleviate the price crunch facing consumer electronics. Everything from phones and laptops, to VR headsets and gaming handhelds have seen price increases due to the RAM shortage.
history.author: Terrence O’Brien
history.descriptionZh:
根据《日经亚洲》报道，尽管供应商正在提升DRAM产能，但预计到2027年底制造商仅能满足60%的市场需求。SK集团董事长甚至表示，短缺状况可能持续至2030年。全球三大内存制造商——三星、SK海力士和美光——均在积极扩充晶圆厂产能，但这些新增产能至少要等到2027年甚至2028年才能投产。SK海力士虽于今年2月在清州开设了新工厂，但这仅是三大厂商中唯一确认在2026年前落地的增产项目。

分析显示，若要在2027年前满足市场需求，2026-2027年间年产能需提升12%。然而根据Counterpoint Research数据，当前厂商的年度产能规划增长率仅为7.5%。值得注意的是，新建产能将主要聚焦于人工智能数据中心所需的高带宽内存（HBM）生产。由于企业已普遍将HBM产能优先于计算机和手机使用的通用DRAM，这些新工厂能在多大程度上缓解消费电子领域的内存价格压力仍存疑问。目前从手机、笔记本电脑到VR头显和游戏掌机，所有消费电子设备均已因内存短缺出现价格上涨现象。
